name: princess
title: Princess
description: Princess is the heart of the story -- a dog with an extraordinary gift for chess and an even bigger gift for charming everyone she meets.
traits: Beginner chess player with natural instincts | Loyal, obedient, and endlessly curious | A people-dog: thrives on attention, affection, and treats | Known to "nudge" anyone who dares stop petting her
background: Princess is half Australian Cattle Dog, half Pit Bull. She lives with the main character in Checker Town and accompanies him on every adventure -- from the local bank (where she is, without question, the Star of the Show) to the long road toward Chess City.
story_role: Princess is the reason everything happens. The journey to Chess City begins because of her, and it is her loyalty and curiosity that keep the story moving forward even when the odds are stacked against the both of them.

name: narrator
title: The Narrator
description: The story's guide and driving force. A young man from Checker Town with big dreams, a strong work ethic, and a deep bond with his dog, Princess.
traits: Late teens | Anime protagonist energy: determined and always moving forward | Ambitious and hopeful, but grounded in the hard reality of Checker Town | Talks with his hands a lot | Has a knack for getting people genuinely excited about things
background: He was born and raised in Checker Town, a place where hope is in short supply and most people never leave. He has his sights set on Chess City -- not just as a destination, but as proof that a better life is possible. His path there is not straightforward: work, sacrifice, and eventually being sent away all stand between him and where he wants to go.
story_role: The narrator is the lens through which we experience Checker Town, Chess City, and the world in between. His relationship with Princess is the emotional core of the series.

name: rival
title: The Rival
description: Introduced as an antagonist, the Rival turns out to be one of the most important allies in the story -- someone who understands the pull of Chess City better than almost anyone.
traits: Same age as the narrator | Intensely driven by chess -- it is his only road back to his family | Tough exterior that softens once common ground is found | A skilled teacher; introduces Princess to the Pirc Defense
background: He lives in Checker Town, but his entire family is in Chess City. That separation is the engine behind everything he does. Every game he plays, every lesson he gives, is a step closer to being reunited with the people he loves. He and the narrator start as rivals but find that they want the same things.
story_role: When the narrator is sent away, the Rival steps up -- taking in Princess and continuing her chess education. His arc moves from adversary to unlikely cornerstone of the group.

name: best-friend
title: The Best Friend
description: The narrator's oldest friend -- and in many ways, his greatest obstacle. Not through malice, but through the quiet weight of complacency.
traits: Has known the narrator since pre-school | Uninspired, or simply content with life in Checker Town | Makes fun of the narrator's ambitions, driven by his own insecurity | Ultimately ends up aligned with the Rival, though for different reasons
background: Growing up together in Checker Town, he and the narrator were inseparable. But where the narrator looks outward toward Chess City, the Best Friend looks inward -- comfortable with what is, skeptical of what could be. When the narrator is sent away, he and the Rival form an unlikely team, each taking in one of the dogs left behind.
story_role: The Best Friend represents the pull of staying put -- the voice that says the dream isn't worth chasing. His presence gives the narrator's journey its emotional stakes: leaving means leaving him behind too.

name: argus
title: Argus
description: Dependable and steady, Argus is the dog you want in your corner when the road gets long and the stakes get real.
traits: Responsible and reliable -- the kind of dog who keeps things together | Picks up chess slowly but earnestly, and eventually becomes decent | Travels with the main crew throughout the journey | Plays as a pawn in games -- not glamorous, but always where he needs to be
background: Argus has always been more follower than leader, but the kind of follower a crew cannot function without. He keeps his head down, does his part, and shows up every time. The journey to Chess City pulls him beyond anything he has done before, and he rises to meet it.
story_role: Argus represents steady loyalty -- the backbone of any good team. His gradual growth as a chess player mirrors his growth as a character: not flashy, but real.

name: oskar
title: Oskar
description: Oskar cannot play chess. He has tried, repeatedly, with great enthusiasm and consistently poor results. He keeps showing up anyway.
traits: Comic relief -- reliably in over his head, never discouraged by it | Chess ability ranges from bad to slightly less bad, depending on the day | Travels with the main crew and throws himself into every situation | Plays as a pawn in games -- usually the first one sacrificed, never seems to mind
background: Where Argus is steady, Oskar is chaotic. He joined the crew the way he joins most things -- by simply appearing and refusing to leave. Nobody remembers quite when he became a permanent fixture, but the journey would be quieter without him, and quieter is not always better.
story_role: Oskar is the crew's levity. His willingness to fail loudly and try again makes even the most tense moments breathable. In a story full of ambition and pressure, he is the reminder that showing up matters more than playing it perfectly.
